topic.title: Configuring Project Settings
topic.shortdesc: How to configure workspace and project settings in OpenRoads Designer.

section.title: Setting Up a Workspace
section.p: A workspace defines the standards, seed files, and configuration that apply to your project. Select or create a workspace from the backstage view when opening the application.
Workspaces are stored in the WorkSpace directory. Your administrator may provide a shared workspace for team projects.

section.title: Civil Standards Configuration
section.p: Civil standards control design rules such as road geometry, drainage parameters, and annotation styles. These are defined in the civil standards file associated with your workspace.
To update civil standards, navigate to Settings, then Civil Standards, and select the appropriate standards file for your region or project type.

section.title: Units and Coordinate Systems
section.p: Set working units and coordinate reference systems before beginning design work. Mismatched units between files can cause geometry errors that are difficult to resolve later.
Access unit settings via the Design File Settings dialog. Metric and imperial unit configurations are both supported.

section.title: User Preferences
section.p: User preferences are stored per user account and persist across sessions. Configure display preferences, tool settings, and keyboard shortcuts from the Preferences dialog.
Preferences can be exported and imported to share configurations across team members or to back up settings before an upgrade.
